product.description:
Samsung Galaxy A50 SM-A505U 64GB Single Sim Unlocked Black Smartphone


The Samsung Galaxy A50 (SM-A505G) is a mid-range smartphone that was released in 2019. It offers a blend of features, including a decent display, capable cameras, and a long-lasting battery. While it might not be the latest model in 2025, it could still be a suitable option for those seeking a budget-friendly device with core functionalities.   

Key Features

  • Display: The A50 boasts a 6.4-inch Super AMOLED Infinity-U display with a resolution of 1080 x 2340 pixels. This screen provides vibrant colors and good contrast, making it enjoyable for media consumption and general use.  
  • Processor: It is powered by the Exynos 9610 Octa-core processor, which delivers adequate performance for everyday tasks, multitasking, and casual gaming.  
  • Memory: The A50 comes with 4GB of RAM, ensuring smooth app performance and multitasking.  
  • Storage: It offers 64GB of internal storage, which can be expanded further with a microSD card, providing ample space for your photos, videos, and apps.  
  • Camera: The A50 features a triple rear camera setup, consisting of a 25MP main sensor, an 8MP ultra-wide lens, and a 5MP depth sensor. This setup enables you to capture a variety of shots, from detailed landscapes to portraits with a blurred background. The front-facing camera is also 25MP, ideal for capturing high-resolution selfies.  
  • Battery: The A50 is equipped with a 4,000mAh battery that supports 15W fast charging, allowing you to stay powered up throughout the day.  
  • Software: The phone launched with Android 9 Pie and is upgradable to Android 11 with Samsung's One UI. Keep in mind that software updates might be limited in 2025.  
  • Other Features: The A50 includes an in-display fingerprint sensor, facial recognition, a 3.5mm headphone jack, and supports dual SIM cards.  

Detailed Specifications

  • Display: 6.4-inch Super AMOLED, 1080 x 2340 pixels (~403 ppi density)  
  • Processor: Exynos 9610 (10nm)  
  • CPU: Octa-core (4x2.3 GHz Cortex-A73 & 4x1.6 GHz Cortex-A53)  
  • GPU: Mali-G72 MP3
  • RAM: 4GB   
  • Storage: 64GB, expandable via microSD  
  • Rear cameras: 25MP (wide), 8MP (ultrawide), 5MP (depth)  
  • Front camera: 25MP  
  • Battery: 4,000mAh, 15W fast charging   
  • OS: Android 9 Pie, upgradable to Android 11, One UI 3.1
  • Dimensions: 158.5 x 74.5 x 7.7 mm (6.24 x 2.93 x 0.30 in)
  • Weight: 166 g (5.86 oz)   
  • Build: Glass front (Gorilla Glass 3), plastic back, plastic frame
  • SIM: Dual SIM (Nano-SIM, dual stand-by)  
  • Connectivity: Wi-Fi, Bluetooth 5.0, GPS, NFC, USB Type-C 2.0  
  • Sensors: In-display fingerprint sensor, accelerometer, gyro, proximity, compass

product.description:
Samsung Galaxy A50 SM-A505U 64GB Single Sim Unlocked Black Smartphone


The Samsung Galaxy A50 (SM-A505G) is a mid-range smartphone that was released in 2019. It offers a blend of features, including a decent display, capable cameras, and a long-lasting battery. While it might not be the latest model in 2025, it could still be a suitable option for those seeking a budget-friendly device with core functionalities.   

Key Features

  • Display: The A50 boasts a 6.4-inch Super AMOLED Infinity-U display with a resolution of 1080 x 2340 pixels. This screen provides vibrant colors and good contrast, making it enjoyable for media consumption and general use.  
  • Processor: It is powered by the Exynos 9610 Octa-core processor, which delivers adequate performance for everyday tasks, multitasking, and casual gaming.  
  • Memory: The A50 comes with 4GB of RAM, ensuring smooth app performance and multitasking.  
  • Storage: It offers 64GB of internal storage, which can be expanded further with a microSD card, providing ample space for your photos, videos, and apps.  
  • Camera: The A50 features a triple rear camera setup, consisting of a 25MP main sensor, an 8MP ultra-wide lens, and a 5MP depth sensor. This setup enables you to capture a variety of shots, from detailed landscapes to portraits with a blurred background. The front-facing camera is also 25MP, ideal for capturing high-resolution selfies.  
  • Battery: The A50 is equipped with a 4,000mAh battery that supports 15W fast charging, allowing you to stay powered up throughout the day.  
  • Software: The phone launched with Android 9 Pie and is upgradable to Android 11 with Samsung's One UI. Keep in mind that software updates might be limited in 2025.  
  • Other Features: The A50 includes an in-display fingerprint sensor, facial recognition, a 3.5mm headphone jack, and supports dual SIM cards.  

Detailed Specifications

  • Display: 6.4-inch Super AMOLED, 1080 x 2340 pixels (~403 ppi density)  
  • Processor: Exynos 9610 (10nm)  
  • CPU: Octa-core (4x2.3 GHz Cortex-A73 & 4x1.6 GHz Cortex-A53)  
  • GPU: Mali-G72 MP3
  • RAM: 4GB   
  • Storage: 64GB, expandable via microSD  
  • Rear cameras: 25MP (wide), 8MP (ultrawide), 5MP (depth)  
  • Front camera: 25MP  
  • Battery: 4,000mAh, 15W fast charging   
  • OS: Android 9 Pie, upgradable to Android 11, One UI 3.1
  • Dimensions: 158.5 x 74.5 x 7.7 mm (6.24 x 2.93 x 0.30 in)
  • Weight: 166 g (5.86 oz)   
  • Build: Glass front (Gorilla Glass 3), plastic back, plastic frame
  • SIM: Single SIM 
  • Connectivity: Wi-Fi, Bluetooth 5.0, GPS, NFC, USB Type-C 2.0  
  • Sensors: In-display fingerprint sensor, accelerometer, gyro, proximity, compass

What is GSM and CDMA?

The majority of carriers, such as AT&T and T-Mobile, use Global System for Mobile communication (GSM) technology. GSM phones have a SIM card that stores your account information and, if you choose, your phone book. When you switch to a new phone, you can activate it by simply removing the card from inside the old phone and inserting it into the new one. Some major carriers, such as Sprint and Verizon networks, mainly use Code Division Multiple Access (CDMA) technology. These phones do not require a SIM card and are activated directly by the carrier.

What is an Unlocked cellphone?

Most cell phones are designed to use a SIM card which is issued by a carrier and provides cell service by activating any phone into which it is inserted. A locked phone will only recognize a SIM card from a particular carrier. The "lock" is a software setting that keeps the cell phone "loyal" to one carrier. If the cell phone is unlocked, it will recognize a SIM card from any carrier. An unlocked cellphone can work with most SIM cards and/or GSM service providers. It also allows you to switch carriers without having to buy a new phone.

Reviews.comment: Phone arrived sealed and in perfect condition. Camera quality is outstanding and battery lasts all day.
Excellent performance and display. A bit pricey but the S Pen and DeX features justify it.
Very fast and smooth. The size is big but manageable. Mystic Bronze looks premium.
Great hardware but I experienced occasional heating during heavy gaming sessions.
Good overall but expected better battery life for such a flagship device.
Camera is fantastic but the phone felt bulky and the fingerprint sensor was inconsistent.
Had some connectivity issues on certain networks despite being unlocked. Customer support helped but it was a hassle.
Received a device with minor screen flicker. Returned it for a replacement.
Love the 108MP camera and zoom capabilities. Videos look professional in 8K.
Fast charging and wireless charging work well. Build quality feels very premium.
DeX desktop mode is surprisingly useful for light productivity. Smooth experience overall.
Solid flagship phone. A couple of software glitches after updates but those were fixed quickly.
